source.sourcePath: raw/sources/DHF (인허가)/09_HnVUE/92_인증 - FDA/참고샘플 - Aspenview/AspenView Cybersecurity Risk Management Report_Risk Mitigation and Control.pdf

sourceSummary.oneLine: AspenView cybersecurity risk management report section describing risk mitigation controls and residual risk acceptability.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports cybersecurity risk mitigation and residual risk acceptability documentation for AspenView software.
keyPoints: Risk mitigation measures include user access control, authentication, account lockout, session restriction, network authentication, encryption, update controls, logging, and security threat detection.
Authentication controls were strengthened through password complexity, repeated login lockout, and single-session account access.
Authorization controls limit software updates to administrators using manufacturer password validation.
Cryptography controls include DICOM encryption for patient data and SHA-256 encryption for credentials.
Overall residual risk acceptability assessment lists mitigation items RM-RC-01-01 through RM-RC-13 as Acceptable.

evidence.claim: AspenView implements user identification and authentication using unique account ID and password.
evidence.evidenceText: User identification and authentication functions are implemented with ID/PW unique to each account.

evidence.claim: AspenView grants different access authority by account.
evidence.evidenceText: The authority for the information that can be accessed by each account is differentially granted.

evidence.claim: AspenView restricts multiple access to the same account.
evidence.evidenceText: AspenView restricts multiple access to the same account by checking the session information of the account. While a given account is logged in, no other session can login with that account

evidence.claim: AspenView restricts account access after more than five incorrect password entries.
evidence.evidenceText: AspenView detects continuous login attempts using an incorrect PW, and restricts access to the account if the PW is incorrectly entered more than 5 times

evidence.claim: AspenView validates network authentication information for data transmission.
evidence.evidenceText: AspenView checks and validates network authentication information for data transmission. On-device network follows the strict network policy of hospitals

evidence.claim: AspenView sessions terminate when network access is lost during screen saver mode.
evidence.evidenceText: Session on the AspenView will terminate when losing network access during screen saver mode

evidence.claim: AspenView password rules require at least 12 alphanumeric characters and a special character.
evidence.evidenceText: Password rules have been reinforced to require at least 12 alpha-numeric characters and a special character when creating an account.

evidence.claim: AspenView passwords are encrypted using SHA-256 hash protocol and masked during entry.
evidence.evidenceText: Passwords are encrypted using SHA-256 hash protocol. When entering the password, it is displayed as *** characters.

evidence.claim: AspenView can expose only desired ports using Windows OS Firewall configuration.
evidence.evidenceText: AspenView can be configured to expose and active only the ports the user desires using Windows OS Firewall

evidence.claim: AspenView encrypts patient information when transmitting DICOM data files.
evidence.evidenceText: AspenView will encrypt patient information when transmitting as DICOM data files, and is able to decrypt the encrypted data that was uploaded

evidence.claim: AspenView can delete stale data after transmitting medical information when configured by the user.
evidence.evidenceText: Upon user configuration, stale data can be deleted after transmitting medical information from AspenView

evidence.claim: AspenView encrypts sensitive patient information when converting to DICOM files.
evidence.evidenceText: All sensitive patient information on AspenView is encrypted when converting to DICOM files from AspenView

evidence.claim: AspenView product updates are limited to administrators and require a manufacturer password to unzip and install a release.
evidence.evidenceText: Deployment of product updates are limited to only the administrators, and even the admins must have the required password from the manufacturer to be able to unzip and install the new release

evidence.claim: AspenView update files require digital signatures.
evidence.evidenceText: Digital signatures are required on the update files of AspenView

evidence.claim: AspenView records access records, patient information inquiry, data creation, change, and deletion logs daily.
evidence.evidenceText: When accessing a user's medical device, log information such as access records, patient information inquiry, data creation, change, and deletion are thoroughly recorded by AspenView on daily basis

evidence.claim: AspenView works with operating system firewall and anti-virus programs for security threat detection and response.
evidence.evidenceText: AspenView works with the operating system’s default firewall and anti-virus programs to detect alarms, errors, and delays due to security threats. Counter measures are taken by the on-device anti-virus programs that must be installed along with AspenView

evidence.claim: Authentication controls were strengthened through password complexity, login lockout, and single-session enforcement.
evidence.evidenceText: Authentication controls have been significantly strengthened for AspenView by increasing the complexity of password requirement, locking users out after repeated trying to login unsuccessfully, and ensuring that a single account can only have a single session logged into the software.

evidence.claim: Authorization controls support software and data integrity by limiting updates to administrators and password-validated files.
evidence.evidenceText: Authorization controls have been reinforced to ensure that the software integrity and the data integrity can be maintained. Updates to the software can only be made by an administrator, and an admin can only patch the software with a set of files that need to be validated by a password from the manufacturer.

evidence.claim: AspenView cryptography controls protect sensitive data using DICOM encryption and SHA-256 credential encryption.
evidence.evidenceText: All patient data is encrypted using DICOM standards, and credentials are encrypted with SHA-256 method to ensure decryption cannot happen without manufacturer’s decryption methods.

evidence.claim: AspenView event detection and logging records login transactions, navigation history, and file modification and deletion operations daily.
evidence.evidenceText: Traceability of data handled by AspenView have been significantly improved by collecting information on all login transactions, navigation history within the program, modification and deletion operations on files in AspenView are all recorded and organized on a day-to-day basis

evidence.claim: Residual risk mitigation items RM-RC-01-01 through RM-RC-13 are evaluated as Acceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: RM-RC -01-01 5 accept N N Y Acceptable ... RM-RC -13 5 accept N N Y Acceptable

reviewFlags.reason: Authority or jurisdiction is present in metadata as authoritySignal FDA and source path, but the provided document text excerpt does not state FDA.
Only pages 20 through 24 of 46 are present in the provided text, so document-wide metadata and context may be incomplete.
Risk control identifier AX-RC-01-07 appears among RM-RC identifiers and may be an OCR or source inconsistency.
